links.how_to_create: How to create a status page

landing.hero_headline_2: at a third of Atlassian's price.
landing.hero_sub: Custom-domain status page plus uptime monitoring in one SaaS. Open source under the hood. From $9/mo founder's price.
landing.hero_microcopy: No credit card · status.yourcompany.com · MIT-licensed stack
landing.how_heading: From zero to page in 60 seconds
landing.how_sub: No install scripts, no agents, no YAML. Three clicks and you're watching production.
landing.step_register_body: Pick an email, a password, and the slug for your public status page. Done in 20 seconds.
landing.step_monitor_body: Paste a URL. We start checking on the next tick — HTTP, status code, body keyword, TLS validity.
landing.step_channel_body: Telegram bot, SMTP, or webhook. A failed check fires after your threshold, with severity and runbook link.
landing.feature_status_page_body: Point status.yourcompany.com at us via a CNAME. Upload your logo, pick your accent colour, ship a status page that looks like part of your product.
landing.feature_uptime_body: Investigating → identified → monitoring → resolved. Post narrative updates and your customers see the timeline render in real time.
landing.feature_atlassian_title: Migrate from Atlassian in one click
landing.feature_atlassian_body: Paste your Statuspage export, we re-create your components, incidents, and full update history. Under 60 seconds, zero downtime.
landing.feature_widget_body: HTTP, TCP, and DNS checks with keyword matching, status-code validation, and TLS 1.2+ verification — built-in, not a separate product.
landing.feature_self_host_body: If you outgrow the hosted plan (or don't trust it), self-host in one docker-compose command. No feature gates between hosted and self-host.
landing.feature_pro_body: Your customers subscribe to status emails. Drop an SVG badge in your README or a JS widget on your site — every incident pushes a banner automatically.
landing.compare_heading: Why not Atlassian Statuspage or Instatus?
landing.compare_sub: Status pages used to cost $29-100/mo because they bundled with PagerDuty-style incident tooling most indie SaaS don't need. Here's how we stack up.
landing.compare_caption_pre: Sources: vendor pricing pages as of 2026-04. Migration time: paste your Atlassian JSON export into
landing.compare_caption_post: and you're live in under a minute.
landing.code_comment: Create a monitor from CI after every deploy
landing.code_caption_pre: Scoped API keys · Typed OpenAPI spec ·

faq_items.a: Yes. 5 monitors, 1-minute checks, unlimited status pages, and Telegram + email + webhook notifications — all free, no credit card.
Checks run at your configured interval (down to 1 minute). A monitor is only marked down after the configured consecutive-failure threshold, so a single flaky check won't page you.

faq_items.q: Can I embed my status page?
faq_items.a: Every monitor you mark public appears on /status/your-slug. The page is SSR + ISR with a 30-second revalidate — share the URL anywhere, embed it in an iframe, or point your own subdomain at it.

faq_items.q: What happens if PingCast itself goes down?
faq_items.a: The checker is a separate service from the API and dashboard. Alerts keep firing even if the dashboard is unreachable. For full independence, self-host — the stack is a single docker-compose file.
Yes. Every field exposed in the dashboard is available over the REST API, and the database is standard Postgres. You can self-host the whole stack or export whenever you want.

landing.social_proof_heading: Built in public. No logo wall, no "trusted by" fiction.
landing.social_proof_sub: Every feature on this page is backed by open-source code under MIT. Read the handlers, the failure modes, the test suite — judge the product from the source, not the marketing.
landing.cta_heading: Built on a real API, not a marketing website.
landing.cta_sub: Every feature you see in the dashboard is available via a stable JSON API with scoped keys. Integrate pingcast into your tools, CI/CD, and runbooks.

pricing.page_subtitle: Founder's price for the first 100 customers — $9/mo locked forever. Retail $19/mo after that. Self-host MIT-free, always.
pricing.pro_subtitle: Branded status pages for paying customers
pro_features: CSV export, RSS feed, SVG badge
pricing.self_host_subtitle: MIT-licensed, run it on your own infra

faq.q: What happens after the founder cap?
faq.a: Founder customers keep $9/mo forever. New signups land on the $19 retail price.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Atlassian Statuspage?
faq.a: Yes. Paste your JSON export at /import/atlassian and we re-create monitors, incidents and the full timeline in one transaction. 60 seconds.

faq.q: Is there a free trial of Pro?
faq.a: Free tier is the trial — same checker, public status page (with our badge). Upgrade once you need branding or custom domain.
Audience carve-outs (different notifications to different customer tiers) — on the roadmap. SLA reports with contractual math — stay on Atlassian if you need them.

auth.register_sub: Free tier is permanent. Upgrade to Pro any time for branded status pages and custom domains.
auth.register_slug_help: Becomes pingcast.io/status/your-slug. Letters, numbers and hyphens, 3–30 characters.

errors.duplicate_email: An account with this email already exists.
errors.duplicate_slug: This slug is taken. Pick another.
errors.weak_password: Password too short — at least 8 characters.

dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.monitors_empty: No monitors yet. Add your first one to start checking uptime.
dashboard.getting_started_sub: A few quick steps to wire up your first alert. This goes away once you're fully set up.
dashboard.getting_started_step_2: Add a notification channel (Telegram or webhook)
dashboard.getting_started_step_3: Bind the channel to your monitor so alerts reach you

dashboard_branding.title: Status-page branding
dashboard_branding.subtitle: Customise how your public status page looks. Pro feature.
dashboard_branding.logo_help: PNG/SVG hosted somewhere public (e.g. your CDN). Empty to remove.
dashboard_branding.accent_help: Hex, e.g. #3b82f6. Used for buttons and highlights.
dashboard_branding.footer_help: Shown at the bottom of your public page. Markdown not supported, plain text only.
dashboard_branding.pro_required_sub: Upgrade to customise your status page with your logo, brand colour, and custom footer.

dashboard_custom_domain.title: Custom domains
dashboard_custom_domain.subtitle: Point status.yourcompany.com at PingCast. We issue and renew the TLS cert automatically.
dashboard_custom_domain.hostname_help: Apex domains (yourcompany.com) won't work — use a subdomain.
dashboard_custom_domain.added: Domain requested. Follow the validation steps.
dashboard_custom_domain.instructions_intro: Two DNS records, then we'll verify.
dashboard_custom_domain.instructions_cname_value: Add a CNAME from your hostname to status.pingcast.io.
dashboard_custom_domain.remove_confirm: Remove this domain? The status page will fall back to the canonical pingcast.io URL.
dashboard_custom_domain.pro_required: Custom domains are a Pro feature.

dashboard_groups.title: Monitor groups
dashboard_groups.subtitle: Group monitors on your public status page (e.g. "API", "Dashboard", "Infrastructure"). Pro feature.
dashboard_groups.list_empty: No groups yet. Add one to organise monitors on the public page.
dashboard_groups.delete_confirm: Delete this group? Monitors keep working but become ungrouped.
dashboard_groups.assign_help: Pick which monitors live in this group. Unassigned monitors render in the "Other" section.

dashboard_maintenance.title: Maintenance windows
dashboard_maintenance.subtitle: Schedule downtime in advance. The status page shows "Planned maintenance" instead of "Down". Alerts still fire to you.
dashboard_maintenance.pro_required: Scheduling maintenance is a Pro feature.

dashboard_subscribers.title: Status-page subscribers
dashboard_subscribers.subtitle: Confirmed email subscribers to your public status page. Pending (unconfirmed) subscriptions don't show — they've either clicked the confirm link or they haven't and don't count. Every outbound email carries a one-click unsubscribe.
dashboard_subscribers.empty_sub: The subscribe box is live on your public status page. When your first customer opts in and confirms, they'll show up here.

dashboard_incidents.title: Incident
dashboard_incidents.body_placeholder: What did you find / fix / observe?

monitors.delete_confirm: Delete this monitor and all its history?

channels.title: Notification channels
channels.subtitle: Telegram, email, or webhook destinations. Bind a channel to a monitor so alerts reach you.
channels.empty: No channels yet. Add Telegram or a webhook to start getting alerts.

api_keys.title: API keys
api_keys.subtitle: API keys let other tools push to and read from PingCast on your behalf. Treat them like passwords.
api_keys.show_once: Copy this key now — it won't be shown again.

import_atlassian.title: Import from Atlassian Statuspage
import_atlassian.subtitle: Paste your Statuspage JSON export. We'll create monitors, incidents, and the timeline in one transaction.
import_atlassian.drop_label: Drop your JSON file here, or click to choose
import_atlassian.pro_required: Atlassian import is a Pro feature.

seo_software.metaTitle: Status page software for SaaS — PingCast ($9/mo)
seo_software.metaDesc: Compact guide to picking status page software: what to look for, what to avoid, and why PingCast ships a branded status page + uptime monitoring for $9/mo.
seo_software.h1: Status page software, minus the $29 entry fee.
seo_software.intro: A public status page is the cheapest trust-building tool a SaaS can ship. It tells your customers you know when something's broken, you're on it, and you'll tell them when it's fixed. This page is the short version of how to pick one.
seo_software.h2_features: What good status page software actually ships
seo_software.features_intro: Ignore pixel animations. The five things that actually matter:
features: status.yourcompany.com via CNAME, with a TLS cert we issue automatically. Without this the status page feels like a third-party tool.
Your logo, your accent colour, your footer. No vendor watermark. Customers should feel they're still on your site.
Incident updates with a state timeline
Investigating → identified → monitoring → resolved, with prose body on each step. Flat lists of `monitor down` / `monitor up` are not status pages.
Customers opt in once, get notified on every state change, unsubscribe in one click. CAN-SPAM / 152-ФЗ want a real double opt-in, not a checkbox.
SVG badge for your README + a JS widget that pops an in-site banner when an incident is open. Free viral distribution.
seo_software.price_body: Status page software is a fixed-cost tool you pay for forever. Every $20 a month is $240/year, $1,200 over five years. Atlassian Statuspage starts at $29/mo; Instatus and Statuspal land at $20-46. PingCast opens at $9/mo (founder's price for the first 100 customers, $19/mo retail after), and self-hosting is free under MIT if you ever want out of the hosted flow.
seo_software.h2_skip: Skip the ones that aren't status pages
seo_software.skip_body: A generic dashboard showing "service up" / "service down" pixels isn't a status page. UptimeRobot, for instance, ships a public dashboard — fine for internal visibility, but there's no incident narrative, no subscribers, no custom domain. Check for the five features above before committing.
seo_software.cta_box_h: Spin up a status page in 60 seconds
seo_software.cta_box_body: Free tier works on pingcast.io/status/your-slug. Upgrade to Pro and point status.yourcompany.com at us.

seo_best.metaTitle: Best status page software in 2026 — ranked + compared
seo_best.metaDesc: Six status page tools ranked by price, branding quality, uptime integration, and self-host availability. PingCast leads at $9/mo; Atlassian still strong at the enterprise end.
seo_best.intro: Ranked by what indie SaaS and mid-market actually need: branded UI, real incident updates, fair pricing, and an escape hatch. Six tools, one recommendation per budget bracket.
seo_best.cta_box_body: You can spend a week comparing, or spin PingCast up in 60 seconds. If it's not right, self-host on your own infra for free or cancel — there's no annual lock-in.

ranked.name: PingCast
ranked.tagline: Best overall: branded status page + uptime monitoring + MIT self-host, $9/mo.
ranked.verdict: Cheapest real-featured option. Incident updates with state timeline, custom domain, Atlassian importer, SVG badge, JS widget — all in one. Open source under MIT.

ranked.name: Atlassian Statuspage
ranked.tagline: Enterprise default — comprehensive, expensive, not sold in Russia.
ranked.verdict: The incumbent. Deep SLA reports, subscriber audiences, built-in Jira workflows. Overkill for most indie SaaS and 3x the price of a PingCast that matches on every customer-facing feature.

ranked.name: Instatus
ranked.tagline: Flashy UI, no monitoring, Pro tier 2.2x the price.
ranked.verdict: Lovely animations and design polish. Loses on the feature count: no uptime monitoring, no Atlassian import, no embeddable JS widget.

ranked.name: Openstatus
ranked.verdict: Great OSS work. AGPL complicates commercial self-host; hosted is the most expensive of the real contenders.

ranked.name: Uptime Kuma
ranked.tagline: Self-host-only, clunky public page, requires Docker skills.
ranked.verdict: Dominates the free-self-host niche. The public status page is functional but nothing you'd put in front of enterprise customers. No hosted tier — if you want someone else to run it, PingCast is the closest spiritual sibling.

ranked.name: UptimeRobot
ranked.tagline: Monitoring-first, not really a status page tool.
ranked.verdict: Generous free tier (50 monitors), but the status page is a dashboard, not a branded customer touchpoint. Keep for internal alerts; pair with PingCast for the public page.

seo_howto.metaTitle: How to create a status page for your SaaS (complete guide)
seo_howto.metaDesc: Step-by-step guide to shipping a branded status page for your SaaS — from domain setup to incident workflows. Uses PingCast as the example, transferable to any tool.
seo_howto.crumb: How to create a status page
seo_howto.h1: How to create a status page for your SaaS
seo_howto.intro: A status page done right takes under an hour of setup and pays back every time something breaks. Here's the full sequence, example tool agnostic (uses PingCast to make it concrete; translates 1:1 to Atlassian, Instatus, Openstatus).
seo_howto.cta_box_h: Want a status page in 60 seconds?
seo_howto.cta_box_body: PingCast is $9/mo (founder's price for the first 100 customers) and ships every step above — domain, subscribers, templates, badge, widget — out of the box.

steps.title: Pick the URL your customers will remember
steps.body: Use status.yourcompany.com. It signals ownership, avoids looking like a third-party tool, and Google indexes it as part of your domain. If you don't have a root domain budget, fall back to a path on your apex (yourcompany.com/status) but avoid subdomains on the vendor (yourcompany.pingcast.io works, but loses the trust-signal).

steps.title: Decide what goes on the page
steps.body: Aim for 3–8 monitored surfaces. More than that becomes a wall of green dots that people stop reading. Common groupings: public-facing app (www), customer API (api.), admin/dashboard, checkout + billing, third-party dependencies you want to surface (Stripe, DB provider).

steps.title: Configure the monitors
steps.body: HTTP with a 30-60s interval is right for nearly everything. Set alert_after_failures: 2 — a single flaky check from a transient DNS hiccup shouldn't page you. For customer-facing APIs, check a representative endpoint that exercises DB, cache, and auth in one call (not just /health — that tells you nothing).

steps.title: Write your incident-update templates now, not at 3 AM
steps.body: Draft canned phrasing for each state transition while you're calm. Four templates cover most incidents: investigating ("We're aware of elevated error rates on <surface>. Investigating now."), identified ("Cause identified: <plain-English cause>. Mitigation in progress."), monitoring ("Fix deployed. Watching for recovery; will confirm in 10-15 minutes."), resolved ("All services recovered. Total incident duration: <mm> minutes. Post-mortem: <link or TBD>.").

steps.title: Enable email subscribers
steps.body: Double opt-in is the only legal form of this (GDPR, CAN-SPAM, 152-ФЗ). The subscribe box goes on your public status page; subscribers confirm via email and get notified on every state change. Unsubscribe link must be one click in every outbound email — no confirmation modals.

steps.title: Drop the badge and widget into your product
steps.body: An SVG status badge in your README signals "this product has its shit together" to anyone looking at your GitHub. A JS widget pops an in-product banner during active incidents so customers don't have to navigate to the status page to find out.

steps.title: Test it before you need it
steps.body: Once, manually post a fake incident in working hours, watch every channel fire (email, Telegram, widget banner), then resolve it. You want to know the notification path works before production breaks at 3 AM.

seo_atlassian_pricing.metaDesc: Atlassian Statuspage pricing tiers — Hobby, Starter, Growth, Enterprise — with what you actually get at each level. Plus the $9/mo PingCast alternative that covers 90% of the Growth-tier features.
seo_atlassian_pricing.intro: Public pricing pages aren't always clear about what jumps between tiers. Here's the breakdown, then the short version of when it makes sense vs when a $9/mo alternative does the same job.
seo_atlassian_pricing.h2_what_get: What you actually get at each tier
seo_atlassian_pricing.h2_when: When PingCast replaces Atlassian — and when it doesn't
we_cover_items: Uptime monitoring built in (Atlassian needs you to BYO)
MIT self-host escape hatch if you outgrow hosted
stay_items: Built-in SLA reports with downtime-bucket math
seo_atlassian_pricing.cta_box_body: Import your Atlassian Statuspage in one click. If you end up needing the Growth tier later, you can always move back — your data exports cleanly.

tiers.name: Hobby
tiers.bestFor: Side projects that don't need a custom domain.

tiers.name: Starter
tiers.bestFor: Solo-founder SaaS wanting a branded page.

tiers.name: Growth
tiers.bestFor: Mid-market SaaS with tiered customer communications.

seo_open_source.metaDesc: PingCast is an open-source status page + uptime monitor under the MIT license. Self-host via docker-compose in one command, or use the hosted tier at $9/mo.
seo_open_source.h1: An open-source status page you can actually trust
seo_open_source.intro: PingCast is MIT-licensed on GitHub and ships with a hosted SaaS tier. Same codebase. Same feature set. Either run it yourself or let us run it — the commercial escape hatch most indie SaaS founders actually want.
seo_open_source.mit_body: Openstatus is AGPL. Uptime Kuma is MIT. Cachet is BSD-3. Licensing matters because it dictates whether you can self-host inside a commercial product without publishing every modification. PingCast picks MIT — fork, patch, embed, redistribute, whatever. Your legal team's easiest call.
seo_open_source.self_body: Four containers (~150 MB total): Go API, Next.js web, Postgres, Redis + NATS. Add one Traefik label for TLS and you're in production.
features: Branded status pages (custom domain in hosted)
seo_open_source.cta_box_body: Both paths work forever. Start hosted; switch to self-host when you outgrow. Or start self-hosted; move to hosted when managing containers stops being fun.

seo_saas.metaTitle: Status page for SaaS — PingCast ($9/mo, Atlassian importer)
seo_saas.metaDesc: The status page every growing SaaS needs: custom domain, branded UI, incident timeline, email subscribers, uptime monitoring built in. $9/mo founder's price.
seo_saas.h1: The status page every growing SaaS eventually needs
seo_saas.intro: Somewhere between your 10th customer and your 100th, support tickets start asking "is this down?" A public status page answers that question before they ask it — and signals to prospects that you know how to run production.
seo_saas.h2_what_want: What SaaS customers actually want to see

points.title: A URL on your domain
points.body: status.yourcompany.com reads as a company asset. status.pingcast.io/your-slug reads as a third-party tool that might go away.

points.title: The timeline, not just the light
points.body: Green/red monitors tell them something broke. An incident timeline tells them you saw it, named it, deployed a fix, and verified recovery — that's trust.

points.title: Opt-in email updates
points.body: Customers who want to know ping-by-ping will subscribe. Customers who'd rather not get spammed won't. You don't get to force the choice.

points.title: The badge in your README
points.body: Developer customers pin an SVG status badge next to your GitHub link. Free public proof of uptime.

stages.body: Free tier. 5 monitors, basic status page at pingcast.io/status/your-slug. Telegram alerts to your phone when things break. Costs nothing.
Pro at $9/mo. Custom domain (status.yourcompany.com), branded UI (your logo and colour), incident updates, email subscribers. The full SaaS-grade look.
Stay on Pro or self-host under MIT for full data sovereignty. At 500+ customers the $108/year cost is invisible; the migration path exists regardless.

seo_saas.cta_box_h: Get it right before you need it
seo_saas.cta_box_body: The wrong time to set up a status page is during an incident. Spin one up now, leave it quietly operational, and it's there when you need it.

seo_template.metaTitle: Status page template — copy-paste incident-update phrasing
seo_template.metaDesc: Canned incident-update copy for each state (investigating / identified / monitoring / resolved). Plus structural templates for the public page itself.
seo_template.intro: Writing incident updates at 3 AM while prod is on fire is a bad time to find your voice. Draft these phrases once, keep them handy, fill the blanks when things break.
seo_template.structure_intro: A good status page has four sections, in this order:
structure: all-green or an active incident summary. First thing anyone sees.
3–8 monitored surfaces, each with a status chip + 90-day uptime %.
timeline for any unresolved issue, with latest state on top.
email-update opt-in. Footer links to an RSS feed and your terms.
seo_template.structure_outro: This is exactly what PingCast renders at /status/<your-slug>. No layout work required on your side.
seo_template.cta_box_h: Skip the design, keep the templates
seo_template.cta_box_body: PingCast's status-page UI is already set. Copy the incident templates above into your runbook and you're ready for the next outage.

templates.label: Initial post — just saw it, don't know why yet
templates.body: We're seeing elevated error rates on <service/surface>. Our team is investigating. We'll post an update within 15 minutes.

templates.label: Initial post — we paged a human
templates.body: We've received alerts that <service> is not responding for some users. An engineer is investigating. We'll update as we learn more.

templates.label: Cause found, fix in progress
templates.body: Cause identified: <plain-English summary — e.g. 'a bad config push to our API gateway caused requests to time out'>. Mitigation is in progress. ETA to recovery: <X minutes>.

templates.label: Third-party dependency
templates.body: The issue is a partial outage at <upstream provider>. We're seeing <X>% error rate on <surface>. We've opened a ticket with them and are exploring a workaround. See also: <upstream status URL>.

templates.label: Fix deployed, watching
templates.body: Fix deployed. Error rates are returning to baseline. We'll continue monitoring for the next 15 minutes and mark this resolved if everything holds.

templates.label: Clean recovery
templates.body: All services have recovered. Total incident duration: <MM> minutes. We'll publish a post-mortem within the next 48 hours at <blog URL or TBD>. Sorry for the disruption.

templates.label: Partial / lingering effects
templates.body: Primary services recovered at <HH:MM UTC>. <Customers with X configuration> may still see stale data until <Y>; this will resolve on its own. Post-mortem to follow.
